betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6BTX§f

§aThe key aromatic feedstock — fraction reformate to produce BTX for hard plastic and fullerene!§f

BTX (Benzene, Toluene, Xylene) is a flammable, combustible, viscous, polluting liquid with a high fuel grade, providing §e3.15 MTU§f and §e7.87 MHE§f per bucket. It is produced by fractionating §bReformate§f in the §bFractioning Tower§f. BTX is the single most important precursor for §bHard Plastic§f production and is also used in §bFullerene§f processing.

§6Production§f:
§b• Fractioning Tower§f (Reformate): §e100mB§f Reformate → §e60mB§f BTX + §e40mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f (Desulfurized Naphtha): §e100mB§f → §e60mB§f BTX + §e40mB§f High-Octane Diesel.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Hard Plastic§f (Chemical Plant): §e500mB§f BTX + §e500mB§f Phosgene → §e1×§f Hard Plastic Bar (requires §bPolycarbonate Synthesis§f template).
§b• Fullerene§f (Ore Acidizer): §e1,000mB§f BTX + §e4×§f Fullerene → §e1×§f Crystalline Fullerite.
§b• Catalytic Cracking Tower§f: §e100mB§f + §e200mB§f Steam → §e80mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons + §e20mB§f Petroleum Gas.
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e100mB§f → §e1×§f Petroleum Coke + §e10mB§f Coker Gas.
§b• Industrial Solidification§f: §e570mB§f → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.

§eTip§f: BTX is the primary gate to §bHard Plastic§f, which is required for the §bCatalytic Converter§f, which in turn enables the §bCatalytic Reformer§f and §bHydrotreater§f. Keep a steady BTX supply by dedicating a Fractioning Tower to Reformate processing!
